title: [main.c:117-119] init_standalone_lora late failure doesn't clean up RX callback
description:
Category not checked: Cleanup completeness on error paths

Bug found: In init_standalone_lora():
1. Line 97: lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback(lora_rx_handler, NULL) - registers callback
2. Line 99-103: lichen_lora_l2_start() called
3. Line 106-109: if get_eui64 fails, calls lichen_lora_l2_stop() 

The issue: lichen_lora_l2_stop() clears the callback (lora_l2.c:434-435), so this is actually fine for the eui64 failure case.

But there's another path:
- Line 117-119: if lichen_log_link_local_from_eui64() fails, we return ret without stopping LoRa.

So after a late failure in IPv6 address logging, LoRa is still running with the RX callback registered. This is inconsistent with the eui64-failure path.

Impact: P4 - Inconsistent cleanup: LoRa continues running after IPv6 address logging failure.
close_reason: Fixed: Code already has goto cleanup label at line 118 that properly calls lichen_lora_l2_stop() and lichen_lora_l2_deinit(). Issue appears to reference an older version of the code.
